# Roam Live

> Free open-source Android IRL streaming app for Twitch, Kick, YouTube, or any RTMP server. Built to stay cool and battery-efficient.

## About

Roam Live is a hobbyist-built Android app for streaming from a phone to live streaming platforms. It's designed for IRL (In Real Life) streaming: walking around with the phone clipped to a vest, on a gimbal, or held in hand, streaming to Twitch, Kick, YouTube, or any RTMP-compatible service.

It's deliberately platform-agnostic. The streamer pastes their full RTMP URL (server + key) and Roam pushes to whatever's on the other end. No Twitch-specific or Kick-specific code paths.

The project exists because iOS IRL streamers have Moblin (open-source, lightweight, well-loved) and Android doesn't have an equivalent. Roam Live aims to be the Android-side answer to that gap.

## Key differentiator

Roam is **lightweight by design**, intentionally. The biggest complaint IRL streamers have about other Android streaming apps (Streamlabs Mobile in particular) is that they make the phone overheat and chew through battery. Multiple users have told us they had to switch to a slower charger to stop their phone overheating while streaming on Streamlabs. Roam runs cool enough that you can fast-charge during a stream. No Google Play Services, no telemetry, no embedded SDKs, no background services running when not streaming.

## Key features

- Works with any RTMP / RTMPS server (no platform lock-in)
- Native chat panel showing Twitch and Kick chat side by side, visible only to the streamer and not burned into the broadcast
- Auto-reconnect when the network drops mid-stream, with configurable max retry time
- Stealth mode: black screen for discretion in public, stream keeps going
- BRB screen with custom text, one-tap mute of camera and audio
- Torch / flashlight toggle for night streams
- Predictive thermal management: warns the streamer and reduces bitrate before the phone overheats and the OS kills the stream

## Privacy

- No telemetry, no analytics, no tracking
- Google Play Services not required to run
- Talks only to the streaming server the user points it at
- Open source under GPL-3.0 so the code can be audited

## Technical

- Platform: Android 10 and up (Android 14+ recommended for full feature set)
- License: GPL-3.0
- Source code: https://github.com/whitspce/roamlive
- Website: https://roamlive.app/

## Distribution

- Free, no payment required
- Distributed as APK directly from GitHub releases (not on the Play Store, by design)
- Optional donations supported via Stripe (card) and Bitcoin: https://roamlive.app/support/

## Status

Early alpha. The core stream-from-phone path works reliably; expect bugs and missing features in less-trodden paths. Feedback is wanted, send anything to feedback@roamlive.app or open a GitHub issue.

## Comparison

People sometimes ask how Roam Live compares to:

- **Moblin (iOS)**: Roam is essentially trying to be the Android equivalent of what Moblin is on iOS — open-source, lightweight, IRL-streamer-focused. Moblin doesn't run on Android, hence Roam.
- **IRL Pro**: probably the closest Android peer. IRL Pro is feature-rich and has been around longer; its disconnect-protection is a paid add-on. The trade-off: users we've spoken with say IRL Pro's free tier ends the stream the moment signal blips, which doesn't work for moving IRL streams. Roam has on-device auto-reconnect that re-establishes the stream when network comes back.
- **Streamlabs Mobile**: Streamlabs supports more platforms out of the box and has more integrations, but is closed-source, heavy on battery, and prone to overheating phones. Multiple users have left Streamlabs specifically because of the overheating. Roam is lighter, runs cooler, and is fully open-source.
- **Larix Broadcaster**: Larix is another Android RTMP-agnostic option. It's more configurable for power users (more codec options, SRT support, network bonding) but lacks built-in chat reading and stealth mode.
- **The official Twitch / Kick mobile apps**: those only stream to their own platform. Roam streams to any RTMP server. The official Kick app also lacks chat reading on screen, BRB, torch toggle, and stealth mode, all of which Roam has.
